entries.content:
PART 1—COVERAGE AND
DEFINITIONS (RULE 1)

entries.title:
1.1 Positions and employees affected by the
rules ...
entries.content:
1.1 Positions and employees affected by the
rules in this subchapter.

entries.content: 1.2 Extent of the competitive service.

entries.title: AUTHORITY: 5 U.S.C. 3301, 3302.
entries.content: AUTHORITY: 5 U.S.C. 3301, 3302.

entries.title:
SOURCE: 28 FR 10022, Sept. 14, 1963, unless
otherw...
entries.content:
SOURCE: 28 FR 10022, Sept. 14, 1963, unless
otherwise noted.

entries.title:
§1.1 Positions and employees affected
by the rules...
entries.content:
§1.1 Positions and employees affected
by the rules in this subchapter.

entries.title:
The rules in this subchapter shall
apply to all po...
entries.content:
The rules in this subchapter shall
apply to all positions in the competi-
tive service and to all incumbents of
such positions. Except as expressly pro-
vided in the rule concerned, the rules
in this subchapter shall not apply to
positions and employees in the ex-
cepted service.

entries.title: §1.2 Extent of the competitive service.
entries.content: §1.2 Extent of the competitive service.

entries.title:
The competitive service shall in-
clude: (a) All c...
entries.content:
The competitive service shall in-
clude: (a) All civilian positions in the
executive branch of the Government
unless specifically excepted therefrom
by or pursuant to statute or by the Of-
fice of Personnel Management (here-
after referred to in this subchapter as
OPM) under §6.1 of this subchapter;
and (b) all positions in the legislative
and judicial branches of the Federal
Government and in the Government of
the District of Columbia which are spe-
cifically made subject to the civil serv-
ice laws by statute. OPM is authorized
and directed to determine finally
whether a position is in the competi-
tive service.

entries.title: $1.3 Definitions.
entries.content: $1.3 Definitions.

entries.title:
As used in the rules in this sub-
chapter:
entries.content:
As used in the rules in this sub-
chapter:

entries.title:
(a) Competitive service shall have the
same meanin...
entries.content:
(a) Competitive service shall have the
same meaning as the words “classified
service’, or “classified (competitive)
service’’, or “classified civil service” as
defined in existing statutes and execu-
tive orders.

entries.title:
(b) Competitive position shall mean a
position in ...
entries.content:
(b) Competitive position shall mean a
position in the competitive service.

entries.title:
(c) Competitive status shall mean basic
eligibilit...
entries.content:
(c) Competitive status shall mean basic
eligibility to be noncompetitively se-
lected to fill a vacancy in a competi-
tive position. A competitive status
shall be acquired by career-conditional
or career appointment through open
competitive examination upon satis-
factory completion of a probationary
period, or may be granted by statute,
executive order, or the Civil Service
Rules without competitive examina-
tion. A person with competitive status
may be promoted, transferred, reas-
signed, reinstated, or demoted without
taking an open competitive examina-
tion, subject to the conditions pre-
scribed by the Civil Service Rules and
Regulations.

entries.title:
(d) An employee shall be considered
as being in th...
entries.content:
(d) An employee shall be considered
as being in the competitive service
when he has a competitive status and
occupies a competitive position unless
he is serving under a temporary ap-
pointment: Provided, that an employee
who is in the competitive service at the
time his position is first listed under
Schedule A, B, or C shall be considered
as continuing in the competitive serv-
ice as long as he continues to occupy
such position.

entries.title:
(e) Tenure shall mean the period of
time an employ...
entries.content:
(e) Tenure shall mean the period of
time an employee may reasonably ex-
pect to serve under his current ap-
pointment. Tenure shall be granted and
governed by the type of appointment
under which an employee is currently
serving without regard to whether he
has a competitive status or whether his
appointment is to a competitive posi-
tion or an excepted position.

entries.title: §1.4 Extent of the excepted service.
entries.content: §1.4 Extent of the excepted service.

entries.title:
(a) The excepted service shall include
all civilia...
entries.content:
(a) The excepted service shall include
all civilian positions in the executive
branch of the Government which are
specifically excepted from the require-
ments of the Civil Service Act or from
the competitive service by or pursuant
to statute or by OPM under §6.1 of this
subchapter.

entries.title:
(b) Excepted service shall have the
same meaning a...
entries.content:
(b) Excepted service shall have the
same meaning as the words “‘unclassi-
fied service’, or “unclassified civil
service’, or ‘‘positions outside the
competitive civil service’ as used in
existing statutes and executive orders.
